results.title: Playgrounds for free
results.brief_description: Shows the construction of more than a dozen community built playgrounds throughout Pennsylvania under a special playground program. Shows how to use old tyres, oil drums, cable reels and other used materials. Tells how to assess resources, and puts the problem of vandalism in perspective.

results.title: Playground (TEFC)
results.creator_credit: David Grieve, Torben Strandgaard and University of California, Los Angeles
results.brief_description: Employs live action, still photographs, animation and split frame techniques to show how creative playgrounds provide opportunities for children to build their own play environments and enjoy their creations in a non-regimented atmosphere.

results.title: Play to learn
results.creator_credit: Joe Bas, Peter Jones and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: This film shows how Julius Erdelyi, a teacher at Woodlands School, New Westminster, prepares children for real-life situations outside the classroom - walking through downtown traffic, for instance. He brings in actual traffic lights and follows this up with field trips. The children suffer from varying degrees of being mentally handicapped, but they all obviously welcome such opportunities to learn and grow.

results.title: Play School. The road show
results.brief_description: Five Play School presenters sing songs, tell stories and play games about cars, buses and bikes - and about how to be safe on the road. Presenters include Benita Collings, John Hamblin, Noni Hazelhurst, Simon Burke, George Spartels.

results.title: Play School. Special; Concert
results.brief_description: Appearing in the “Play school special” are eight well-loved Play school presenters: Benita Collings, Merridy Eastman, John Hamblin, Noni Hazelhurst, Jennifer Ludlum, Philip Quast, Don Spencer and John Waters. Favourite songs include ‘Old Macdonald’, ‘Baa baa black sheep’, ‘The paper song’, ‘There’s a bear in there’ and many more. Some of the stories are ‘Harry the dirty dog’, ‘There was a princess’ and ‘The hole’. Benita Collings and John Hamblin perform in a Play School concert. The audience joins in with the words and actions of popular Play school songs. Among them are ‘I’m a little teapot’ and ‘Bananas in pyjamas’.

results.title: Play school. Out and about
results.creator_credit: Henrietta Clark, Maija Rove and ABC-TV (Australia)
results.brief_description: Play School in these two programs goes to the beach and spends a day in the Botanic Gardens. Both programs feature songs and stories about the beach and the botanic gardens. Presenters include Colin Buchanan, John Hamblin, Don Spencer and Monica Trapaga.

results.title: Play school. Dinosaurs
results.brief_description: What was it like when dinosaurs were alive? This fascinating program provides an entertaining way for children to understand more about the prehistoric world. Children will be captivated by stories, activities and songs which tell of a world long ago.

results.title: Platypus cove
results.creator_credit: Peter Maxwell, Geoffrey Gardiner and Independent Productions (AU)
results.brief_description: Jim Mason is a 15 year old street struggling to stay on the right side of the law. He lands a job on a tugboat from Platypus Cove and things seem to be looking up. His peace is shattered however when he becomes the number one suspect for a sabotage attempt on the boat. It’s up to Jim and his crew of friends to catch the real culprits in a race around Sydney harbour. No year of production available.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Peter Maxwell, Geoffrey Gardiner and Independent Productions (AU)

results.title: Plasmo
results.creator_credit: Anthony Lawrence, Tony Wright, Beyond International, Film Victoria and Plasmo Mega Studios
results.brief_description: Plasmo, Parsty and Nik Nik are stuck on the planet Pynco, desperate to return home, when they come across two explorers, Brucho and Coredor. This tape includes seven adventures previously screened on ABC television. Episodes include: PLASMO AND THE MOONTOWER, PLASMO BLASTS OFF, PLASMO IN DEEP SPACE, PLASMO ARRIVES, TOUR OF MONJOTROLDECLIPDOC, PLASMO AND THE SPACE GHOSTS, PLASMO AND THE BOOKWORM. The magic of puppetry and animation used to create Plasmo’s world will delight children everywhere. Voices provided by Abbe Holmes, Pia Morley and Phil Houghton.

results.title: Plas Newydd, Angelsey, Wales
results.brief_description: Plas Newydd, which means “new house”, is the country home of the Marquis of Angelsey, Wales. The film examines this stately home and the present Marquis of Angelsey discusses its history. Part of the series “A Place in Europe”.
